title: Topic, Headers, and Alternate Routing · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs topic, headers, and alternate routing from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a deterministic Go routing and unroutable-decision table after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Topic Segment Design
steps.instruction:
Order the Topic Segment Design RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Topic Segment Design.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Topic Segment Design assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Topic Segment Design, keep Topic Segment Design connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A review Go messaging team handles case 6108 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

In Go, a nil publish error does not establish that any primary queue received the message.

options.text: Declare RabbitMQ and Go outcome, topology, ownership, identity, and byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets for the review Topic Segment Design case
Predict RabbitMQ and Go publish, route, custody, delivery, effect, failure, cancellation, and recovery outcomes for the review Topic Segment Design case
Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the review Topic Segment Design case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the review Topic Segment Design case
